cities.name: Amritsar
cities.overview: Amritsar is the spiritual and cultural capital of Punjab, home to the iconic Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib), the holiest shrine in Sikhism. The city blends profound spirituality, poignant history from the freedom struggle, and vibrant Punjabi culture, cuisine, and hospitality.
cities.history: Founded in 1577 by Guru Ram Das, Amritsar grew around the sacred Amrit Sarovar. It became a major center during the Sikh Empire under Maharaja Ranjit Singh. Sites like Jallianwala Bagh commemorate the 1919 massacre, while the daily Wagah Border ceremony symbolizes Indo-Pak relations.

nearest_airport.name: Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport

places.name: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib)
places.description: The holiest Gurdwara in Sikhism, with its gold-plated sanctum surrounded by the holy Amrit Sarovar. Known for its stunning architecture, langar (community kitchen) serving thousands daily, and atmosphere of peace and equality.
places.tips: Visit at sunrise or during evening aarti for magical lighting. Cover head, remove shoes, and participate in langar for the full experience.

places.name: Jallianwala Bagh
places.description: A memorial garden commemorating the 1919 massacre where British troops fired on unarmed civilians. Features the Martyrs' Well and bullet-marked walls.
places.tips: Combine with a visit to the nearby Partition Museum for deeper historical context.

places.name: Wagah Border Ceremony
places.description: Iconic daily flag-lowering ceremony at the India-Pakistan border featuring high-energy parades by BSF and Pakistan Rangers.
places.tips: Reach early for good seats. Carry ID and arrive via taxi or tour.

places.name: Partition Museum
places.description: The world's first museum dedicated to the 1947 Partition of India. Houses rare artifacts, personal stories, and photographs documenting one of the largest migrations in history.
places.tips: Allow extra time for the emotional exhibits. Audio guides available in multiple languages.

places.name: Gobindgarh Fort
places.description: A historic 18th-century fort built by Maharaja Ranjit Singh. Now a living museum with light-and-sound shows, coin museum, and cultural performances showcasing Sikh heritage.
places.tips: Visit in the evening for the spectacular light show. Great for families and history buffs.

places.name: Durgiana Temple
places.description: A beautiful Hindu temple modeled after the Golden Temple, dedicated to Goddess Durga. Features intricate silver doors and a sacred tank.
places.tips: Visit during evening aarti for a serene experience. Reflects the harmonious blend of Sikh and Hindu traditions in Punjab.

cities.name: Chandigarh
cities.overview: Chandigarh is India's first planned city, designed by Le Corbusier. It serves as the shared capital of Punjab and Haryana, known for its modernist architecture, lush gardens, wide boulevards, and high quality of life.
cities.history: Post-independence, Chandigarh was built in the 1950s as a symbol of modern India. It features unique urban planning with sectors, rock sculptures, and green spaces.

places.name: Rock Garden
places.description: A unique garden made entirely from industrial and household waste, featuring sculptures, waterfalls, and amphitheaters created by Nek Chand.
places.tips: Visit in the morning. Combine with Sukhna Lake for a full day.

places.name: Sukhna Lake
places.description: A man-made lake at the foothills of the Shivaliks, ideal for boating, walking, and evening strolls.
places.tips: Boating is available. Great for sunrise or sunset views.

places.name: Zakir Hussain Rose Garden
places.description: One of Asia's largest rose gardens with over 50,000 rose bushes and 1,600 varieties. Features a beautiful amphitheater and jogging tracks.
places.tips: Best visited during February–March when roses are in full bloom. Perfect for morning walks or picnics.

places.name: Capitol Complex & Open Hand Monument
places.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site designed by Le Corbusier. Includes the Legislative Assembly, High Court, and the iconic Open Hand Monument symbolizing peace.
places.tips: Guided tours available. Photography restricted in some areas — check rules before visiting.

cities.name: Ludhiana
cities.overview: Ludhiana is Punjab's largest city and a major industrial center famous for hosiery, woolens, and manufacturing. It offers a mix of bustling markets, historical sites, and Punjabi rural culture.
cities.history: Founded during the Lodhi dynasty, it grew under Sikh rule and became an industrial powerhouse post-independence.
cuisine: Makki di Roti with Sarson da Saag

places.name: Lodhi Fort (Purana Qila)
places.description: A 15th-century fort built during the Lodhi dynasty on the banks of the Sutlej River. Offers panoramic views and a glimpse into medieval Punjab history.
places.tips: Combine with a visit to the nearby riverbank for a relaxing stroll. Best visited in the morning.

places.name: Maharaja Ranjit Singh War Museum
places.description: A modern museum showcasing Punjab's military history with artifacts, weapons, and exhibits on Maharaja Ranjit Singh and the Sikh Empire.
places.tips: Interactive displays make it engaging for all ages. Ideal for history enthusiasts.

places.name: Nehru Rose Garden
places.description: One of the largest rose gardens in Asia with thousands of rose varieties. Features fountains, an open-air theater, and beautiful landscaping.
places.tips: Visit during the rose blooming season (February–March). Great spot for families and photography.

cities.name: Patiala
cities.overview: Patiala is known for its rich royal heritage, grand palaces, and vibrant culture. It is famous for Patiala peg, phulkari embroidery, and traditional sports.
cities.history: Founded in the 18th century by the Sidhu dynasty, it was a prominent princely state.

places.name: Qila Mubarak Complex
places.description: The majestic royal fort and palace complex of the Patiala rulers. Features Sheesh Mahal, Darbar Hall, and beautiful gardens showcasing royal opulence.
places.tips: Hire a local guide for historical insights. The Sheesh Mahal's mirror work is breathtaking.

places.name: Baradari Gardens
places.description: Beautiful Mughal-style gardens surrounding the Baradari Palace. Known for lush lawns, fountains, and seasonal flowers.
places.tips: Perfect for evening walks or picnics. Combine with a visit to the nearby palace.

places.name: Kali Mata Temple
places.description: A historic temple dedicated to Goddess Kali with a 6-foot statue brought from Bengal. Features intricate architecture and spiritual ambiance.
places.tips: Visit during Navratri for vibrant celebrations. Respectful attire recommended.

cities.name: Anandpur Sahib
cities.overview: Anandpur Sahib is one of the five most holy places in Sikhism, where Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa in 1699.
cities.history: Site of major Sikh historical events and the magnificent Virasat-e-Khalsa museum.

places.name: Virasat-e-Khalsa
places.description: A stunning modern museum dedicated to Sikh history and the birth of the Khalsa. Features immersive galleries, artifacts, and breathtaking architecture.
places.tips: Allow at least 2 hours. Audio guides enhance the experience significantly.

places.name: Takht Sri Kesgarh Sahib
places.description: One of the five Takhts of Sikhism where Guru Gobind Singh Ji founded the Khalsa in 1699. A sacred site with a beautiful sarovar and gurdwara complex.
places.tips: Participate in langar and morning prayers for a spiritual experience. Dress modestly.

cities.name: Jalandhar
cities.overview: Jalandhar is a major commercial city known for sports goods manufacturing, leather goods, and a mix of spiritual and modern attractions.
cities.history: One of the oldest cities in Punjab with roots in ancient times.

places.name: Rangla Punjab Haveli
places.description: A living cultural village showcasing traditional Punjabi lifestyle, folk dances, crafts, and authentic cuisine in a haveli setting.
places.tips: Book in advance for evening cultural shows and dinner. Perfect for experiencing rural Punjab.

places.name: Devi Talab Mandir
places.description: An ancient temple dedicated to Goddess Durga, one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Features a sacred tank and vibrant festivals.
places.tips: Visit during Navratri for grand celebrations. The temple pond is believed to have healing properties.

cities.name: Bathinda
cities.overview: Bathinda is one of Punjab's oldest cities, known for its ancient forts, thermal power plants, and growing industrial base.
cities.history: Home to Qila Mubarak, one of the oldest surviving forts in northern India.

places.name: Qila Mubarak
places.description: One of the oldest forts in northern India, dating back over 1,000 years. A symbol of Bathinda's rich history and strategic importance.
places.tips: Explore the ramparts for panoramic views. Best visited in cooler months.

places.name: Takht Sri Damdama Sahib
places.description: One of the five Takhts of Sikhism where Guru Gobind Singh Ji compiled the Guru Granth Sahib. A peaceful spiritual site with historical significance.
places.tips: Attend the daily langar and prayers. Respectful dress and behavior required.

cities.name: Kapurthala
cities.overview: Kapurthala is famous for its European-style palaces, gardens, and French architectural influence, earning it the nickname 'Paris of Punjab'.
cities.history: Princely state known for Jagatjit Palace and Moorish Mosque.

places.name: Jagatjit Palace
places.description: A magnificent Indo-Saracenic palace built by Maharaja Jagatjit Singh, now housing a Sainik School. Features grand halls and European-inspired design.
places.tips: Exterior views are impressive. Check for permission if interior access is possible.

places.name: Moorish Mosque
places.description: A replica of the famous mosque in Cordoba, Spain, commissioned by Maharaja Jagatjit Singh. Features intricate tile work and minarets.
places.tips: A unique blend of Moorish and Indian architecture. Visit during daylight for best photos.

cities.name: Mohali (SAS Nagar)
cities.overview: Mohali is a planned city adjacent to Chandigarh, known for its cricket stadium, IT parks, and modern infrastructure.
cities.history: Developed as part of the Tricity region in recent decades.

places.name: Punjab Cricket Association IS Bindra Stadium
places.description: A world-class cricket stadium and one of India's premier venues. Hosts international matches and offers tours when no events are scheduled.
places.tips: Check match schedule in advance. Great for sports enthusiasts.

places.name: Fateh Burj
places.description: The tallest victory tower in India, commemorating the Sikh victory in the Battle of Chappar Chiri. Offers panoramic views from the top.
places.tips: Climb the tower for stunning views of the surrounding area. Ideal for history and architecture lovers.

cities.name: Pathankot
cities.overview: Pathankot serves as the main gateway to Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir, with scenic surroundings and military significance.
cities.history: Important cantonment town with historical forts nearby.

places.name: Shahpur Kandi Fort
places.description: A historic fort overlooking the Ravi River with stunning views. Offers insights into the region's medieval past.
places.tips: Combine with a drive along the river for scenic beauty.

cities.name: Hoshiarpur
cities.overview: Hoshiarpur is known for its citrus orchards, woodwork, and ancient temples.
cities.history: Ancient city with strong cultural and spiritual traditions.

places.name: Ancient Temples & Gardens
places.description: Known for its historic temples and lush citrus orchards. A peaceful town offering a glimpse into rural Punjab life.
places.tips: Explore local woodwork markets for souvenirs.

cities.name: Fatehgarh Sahib
cities.overview: Fatehgarh Sahib is a major Sikh pilgrimage site where the younger sons of Guru Gobind Singh attained martyrdom.
cities.history: Site of the historic Gurdwara Sri Fatehgarh Sahib.

places.name: Gurdwara Sri Fatehgarh Sahib
places.description: A sacred Gurdwara marking the martyrdom of the younger sons of Guru Gobind Singh Ji. A place of deep historical and spiritual significance.
places.tips: Participate in langar and prayers. Maintain silence and respect.

cities.name: Faridkot
cities.overview: Faridkot is known for its royal palace, historical gurdwaras, and association with Baba Farid.
cities.history: Named after the Sufi saint Baba Farid.

places.name: Faridkot Royal Palace & Fort
places.description: The historic royal palace and fort associated with the Faridkot royal family. Features beautiful architecture and gardens.
places.tips: Explore the grounds for a sense of royal Punjab history.

cities.name: Rupnagar (Ropar)
cities.overview: Rupnagar is an ancient town on the banks of the Sutlej River, with archaeological significance and proximity to the Shivalik hills.
cities.history: Harappan-era site with later Sikh and Mughal history.

places.name: Sutlej River & Archaeological Sites
places.description: The ancient town on the Sutlej River with Harappan-era ruins and scenic river views. A blend of history and natural beauty.
places.tips: Ideal for a peaceful riverside walk or short archaeological exploration.

cities.name: Sangrur
cities.overview: Sangrur offers a glimpse into rural Punjab with royal history and agricultural landscapes.

places.name: Sangrur Royal Palace & Gardens
places.description: The historic royal palace and surrounding gardens of the former princely state. Reflects the grandeur of Punjab's royal past.
places.tips: Explore the grounds for a quiet cultural experience.
